panels.title: What this dashboard shows in v0.2

options.content:
**v0.2 puts iplane back into the inference data path.**

From Ch 7 onward `iplane serve` mounts a router that operators dial directly: `POST http://<iplane>/v1/chat/completions` with the model in the request body, or `POST http://<iplane>/v1/<deploy-id>/v1/chat/completions` for explicit-deployment dispatch. The router forwards to the engine and instruments each request, restoring the three application metric families the v0.1 dashboard deliberately left empty:

- **Request rate** — `inference.requests.total`, labeled by `deploy_id`, `model`, `status`.
- **Latency** — `inference.request.duration` histogram (bimodal LLM buckets), same labels.
- **Token throughput** — `inference.tokens.generated`, parsed from the engine's `usage` field (works for both non-streaming responses and SSE streams that include a usage frame).

The **provider rate catalog** (bottom) still ticks from the v0.1 catalog source -- carried forward unchanged.

If the panels here are flat, you likely have no traffic flowing yet. `iplane load --target <deploy-id>` (v0.2 cross-cut) or any OpenAI SDK pointed at `http://<iplane>/v1` will populate them.

panels.title: Request rate (req/s, per deploy_id)
panels.description: Rate of inference requests through the router, broken out by deployment and outcome status. The bedrock load metric -- one series per (deploy_id, status). Flat = no traffic; spikes during demos = the router is taking calls.

panels.title: Request latency (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: End-to-end latency observed at the router boundary (includes engine compute + network). Histogram_quantile over the bimodal-tuned buckets from Chapter 6.6.4. Three percentiles aggregated across deploy_ids -- drill into a specific deployment by adding {deploy_id="x"} to the expressions.

panels.title: Completion tokens / sec (per deploy_id)
panels.description: Tokens generated by the engine, parsed from the OpenAI usage field. Counts both non-streaming responses (full body parse) and streaming responses with usage frames (incremental parse). Streams whose client didn't request usage will be undercounted.

panels.title: Provider rate catalog (from providers.yaml)
panels.description: Per-second cost for every provider iplane knows about, converted to $/hr for readability. Same static catalog as the v0.1 dashboard -- carried forward because cost economics doesn't depend on the data plane shape.
